mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show negative mean during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 5.0%, labor market deterioration signals impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.0% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in retail sales and consumer price inflation creates stagflation expectations that force monetary tightening, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when both series rise concurrently, with out-of-sample directional accuracy below 45%.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production confirms supply-side recovery, triggering short-term positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production gains fail to generate positive 5-day forward returns during recovery phases, with mean return less than or equal to 0.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in housing starts signals collapsing residential investment and broader credit stress, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 60-day returns in greater than 60% of periods following a 10% housing starts drawdown.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates robust consumer spending capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for payroll growth versus 20-day forward returns falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation outpaces the federal funds rate, real rates turn negative and policy tightening expectations rise, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal yields positive forward returns in greater than 50% of triggered months, or predictive R-squared remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.0% confirms economic stagnation, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as earnings revisions turn downward.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in greater than 65% of quarters where GDP growth drops below 1.0%.
